id: packages/core/src/token-verify.ts-Hardcoded `notBeforeLeeway = 30` seconds is not configurable-developer-review-f730b854-high
title: packages/core/src/token-verify.ts
description: Hardcoded `notBeforeLeeway = 30` seconds is not configurable. The `TokenVerifier` constructor accepts options but clock skew tolerance is buried as a magic number. Different deployments (cross-region, mobile clients) need different tolerances. Should be exposed via `TokenVerifierOptions`.
**Code duplication: two independent JWT verification implementations**
title: packages/core/src/token-verify.ts vs packages/server/src/lib/auth.ts
id: packages/core/src/token-verify.ts vs packages/server/src/lib/auth.ts-**Code duplication: two independent JWT verification impleme-historian-review-f730b854-high
packages/core/src/config.ts-Wildcard scope normalization inconsistency. `normalizeScope(-developer-review-f730b854-high
description: Wildcard scope normalization inconsistency. `normalizeScope("*")` converts to `"*:*:*:*"`, but `matchScope()` in scope-matcher.ts:58 has a fast-path `granted.includes("*")` that only matches the bare `"*"` string. After config normalization, this fast-path becomes dead code. The normalized form still works via parsed matching, but the inconsistency is confusing and the dead code path suggests the two modules weren't designed together.
title: packages/core/src/config.ts
packages/core/src/file-acl.ts
description: Default-open ACL model is security-sensitive. `checkPermission()` returns `true` (allow) when no enforceable rules are found (`return !enforceableRuleSeen`). While documented as intentional, this means a misconfigured or empty ACL silently grants full access. A "default-deny" model with explicit opt-in for open directories would be safer for a security primitive. At minimum, this should log a warning when falling through to default-open.
id: packages/core/src/file-acl.ts-Default-open ACL model is security-sensitive. `checkPermissi-developer-review-f730b854-high

title: scripts/relay/relay.sh

description: Inconsistent private member conventions. Methods use `_prefixed` names (`_fetchJwks`, `_findKey`, `_verifySignature`, `_validateClaims`) while the class also uses `#parseToken`. Pick one convention — prefer `#` private fields for true encapsulation since `_` prefix is merely a naming convention with no runtime enforcement.

id: packages/core/src/token-verify.ts-Inconsistent private member conventions. Methods use `_prefi-developer-review-f730b854-medium

title: scripts/relay/relay.sh
id: scripts/relay/relay.sh-Hardcoded default secrets: `secret="${SIGNING_KEY:-dev-secre-developer-review-f730b854-medium
description: Hardcoded default secrets: `secret="${SIGNING_KEY:-dev-secret}"` and `DEFAULT_ZERO_CONFIG_SECRET="dev-relay-secret"`. While intended for dev workflows, these known defaults could be deployed to staging/prod if env vars aren't set. Add a runtime guard: if no SIGNING_KEY is set and the command is not explicitly `--dev`, refuse to start.

id: scripts/relay/relay.sh-Large inline Node.js scripts embedded in bash heredocs (35+ -developer-review-f730b854-medium
title: scripts/relay/relay.sh
description: Large inline Node.js scripts embedded in bash heredocs (35+ and 42+ lines respectively). These are untestable in isolation and difficult to debug. Extract into separate `.ts` or `.js` files under `scripts/relay/` so they can be unit tested and linted independently.

id: packages/core/src/__tests__/config.test.ts-Test coverage gaps. Only 4 tests covering the happy path, ro-developer-review-f730b854-medium
title: packages/core/src/__tests__/config.test.ts
description: Test coverage gaps. Only 4 tests covering the happy path, role expansion, unknown roles, and invalid scopes. Missing tests for: empty agents array, duplicate agent names, missing required fields (workspace, signing_secret), circular role dependencies, ACL path traversal attempts (`..`), and empty/whitespace-only scope strings. The `normalizeAclPath` function has path-traversal rejection logic that is untested directly.

description: **Scope parser rejects `*` (universal wildcard) in colon-segmented position but scope-matcher accepts bare `*` in granted list**

title: packages/core/package.json
description: Wildcard dependency `"@relayauth/sdk": "*"`. Should pin to a semver range (e.g., `^1.0.0`) to prevent breaking changes from being silently pulled in. Wildcard deps defeat the purpose of lockfiles for downstream consumers.

packages/core/src/errors.ts
id: packages/core/src/errors.ts-`this.name = new.target.name` dynamically sets error name fr-developer-review-f730b854-low
description: `this.name = new.target.name` dynamically sets error name from the class constructor. If code is minified/bundled, class names change and error names become meaningless. Use explicit string names: `this.name = "InvalidTokenError"` etc.

CLI argument parsing uses bare `args.shift() ?? ""` without validation for `projectDir`, `agentName`, and `workspace`. Empty strings silently propagate, causing cryptic errors later. Add a simple check: `if (!projectDir) { console.error("Usage: ..."); process.exit(1); }`

title: packages/types/src/token.ts
description: `workspace_id` and `agent_name` are optional in `RelayAuthTokenClaims`, but the JWT mapping work in this PR (generate-dev-token.sh) and relayfile integration require them. Consider a separate `RelayFileTokenClaims` type that extends the base with these as required fields, so the type system enforces the contract.
